experiences: Blue T1: Wazuh 커스텀 룰 7개 배포 완료 (100100~100106) — local_rules.xml에 직접 추가

Blue T1: 룰 배포 방법 — echo 1 | sudo -S bash -c 'cat >> /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml << EOF
<group name="custom_web">
<rule id="100100" level="12">
<if_sid>31100</if_sid>
<match>UNION|SELECT</match>
<description>SQL Injection attempt (UNION/SELECT)</description>
<mitre><id>T1190</id></mitre>
</rule>
</group>
EOF'
Blue T1: Suricata 시그니처 sid:1000001 배포 (secu 서버의 /var/lib/suricata/rules/custom.rules)
Blue T1: logtest 검증 성공 — echo '10.20.30.201 GET /?id=-1+UNION+SELECT+1,2,3--' | /var/ossec/bin/wazuh-logtest → rule=100100 트리거 확인
Blue T1: Wazuh agent 버전 불일치 이슈 — web agent 4.14.4 > manager → 등록 거부됨
Blue T1: rsyslog 대안 — web에서 rsyslog로 Apache 로그를 siem으로 전달 설정
Purple: Wazuh manager는 active (running) 상태, 11시간+ uptime
[auto] check the available tools/paths on siem@10.20.30.100: sshpass -p1 ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no siem@10.20.30.100 'echo 1 | sudo -S ls /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json'
[auto] check Wazuh manager status and alerts log: sshpass -p1 ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no siem@10.20.30.100 'echo 1 | sudo -S systemctl status wazuh-manager'

[auto] Attempt SQL injection via login form, correcting syntax error and escaping quotes: curl -s -X POST http://10.20.30.80:3000/rest/user/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "{\"email\":\' OR 1=1--\"

[auto] Attempt SQL injection via login form, correcting syntax error and escaping quotes, trying a different payload: curl -s -X POST http://10.20.30.80:3000/rest/user/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "{\"email\":\'admin\' OR
[auto] Use JWT from step2: curl -s http://10.20.30.80:3000/api/Users: curl -s http://10.20.30.80:3000/api/Users
[auto] Attempt to retrieve user list with a JWT, noting the previous error: curl -s http://10.20.30.80:3000/api/Users -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token> placeholder'
[auto] Attempt to retrieve user list with a JWT, correcting the header format: curl -s http://10.20.30.80:3000/api/Users -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token> placeholder'

daemon_events.action: Review Java command-line arguments for potential vulnerabilities.
daemon_events.report: The provided output shows a Wazuh Indexer process running with several Java command-line arguments. While not inherently malicious, some arguments like `-XX:+ShowCodeDetailsInExceptionMessages` and `-XX:-OmitStackTraceInFastThrow` can expose internal details that could be leveraged in debugging or, potentially, by an attacker. A thorough review of these arguments and their implications within the Wazuh Indexer context is recommended.  Ensure that the arguments are necessary and that their security implications are understood.
